content.content:
1	# API Specification
2	
3	This document defines the REST API surface for the offline-first CivicWorks Community Services backend.
4	It is written to directly implement the business rules captured in `docs/questions.md` (numbered 1-15).
5	
6	## 1. Conventions
7	
8	### Base URL
9	
10	`/api/v1`
11	
12	### Authentication and Authorization (RBAC)
13	
14	* Every request is authenticated and associated with a `user_id` and `role`.
15	* Access is controlled by role:
16	  * `SYSTEM_ADMIN`: global configuration and audits.
17	  * `CONTENT_EDITOR`: create/schedule content.
18	  * `MODERATOR`: comment review and sensitive-word moderation.
19	  * `BILLING_CLERK`: fee setup, billing runs, posting and reversals, reconciliation workflows.
20	  * `DISPATCHER`: zone capacity rules and forced assignments.
21	  * `DRIVER`: accept/decline work with constraints.
22	  * `AUDITOR`: read-only financial and activity reporting.
23	
24	### Idempotency (Offline-safe)
25	
26	Offline clients may retry requests when the network is unavailable or when connectivity resumes.
27	The server must treat the same logical operation as a single action.
28	
29	* For all mutating requests (`POST`, `PATCH`, `PUT`, `DELETE`), clients MUST send:
30	  * `Idempotency-Key: <uuid>`
31	* Server behavior:
32	  * If the same key is seen again for the same authenticated user, return the original result (HTTP 200/201/204/409 as appropriate).
33	  * Idempotency keys MUST be persisted locally (PostgreSQL) so retries remain correct across restarts.
34	
35	### Optimistic Concurrency Control (Conflict Detection)
36	
37	To resolve offline-first conflicts safely, the API uses optimistic locking for critical entities.
38	
39	* Entities that support conflict detection include: `ContentItem`, `Comment`, `Bill`, `Payment`, `DispatchOrder`.
40	* For updates/mutations that change state:
41	  * Client sends `entity_version` in the request body.
42	  * Server compares to current `version` stored in the database.
43	  * On mismatch:
44	    * Return `409 Conflict` with a payload describing the server’s current state and a suggested reconciliation path.
45	
46	### Money Types
47	
48	* Use integer cents everywhere in requests/responses:
49	  * `amount_cents`, `balance_cents`, `fee_cents`, `discount_cents`.
50	* When the UI operates with decimals, convert to cents client-side to avoid floating errors.
51	
52	## 2. Error Model
53	
54	All errors return JSON:
55	
56	```json
57	{
58	  "error_code": "VALIDATION_FAILED",
59	  "message": "Human readable summary",
60	  "details": { }
61	}
62	```
63	
64	Common `error_code` values:
65	
66	* `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`
67	* `VALIDATION_FAILED`
68	* `CONFLICT_VERSION_MISMATCH`
69	* `IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT`
70	* `BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION`
71	* `SCHEDULE_NOT_ALLOWED`
72	
73	## 3. Content and Resource Management
74	
75	Rich-text is stored as sanitized HTML. The server must sanitize and store the final HTML.
76	
77	### 3.1 Create Content Item
78	
79	`POST /content/items`
80	
81	Request:
82	
83	```json
84	{
85	  "type": "NEWS|POLICY|EVENT|CLASS",
86	  "title": "string",
87	  "sanitized_body_html": "<html>...</html>",
88	  "tags": ["string"],
89	  "scheduled_at": "ISO-8601|null",
90	  "entity_version": 0
91	}
92	```
93	
94	Response `201`:
95	
96	```json
97	{
98	  "id": "content_item_id",
99	  "state": "DRAFT|SCHEDULED|PUBLISHED",
100	  "published_at": "ISO-8601|null",
101	  "version": 1
102	}
103	```
104	
105	### 3.2 Query Content Items
106	
107	`GET /content/items?type=&tag=&state=&from=&to=&q=`
108	
109	Supports filtering and full-text search (server-side).
110	
111	### 3.3 Update Content Item
112	
113	`PATCH /content/items/{id}`
114	
115	Request:
116	
117	```json
118	{
119	  "title": "string|null",
120	  "sanitized_body_html": "<html>...</html>|null",
121	  "tags": ["string"]|null,
122	  "scheduled_at": "ISO-8601|null",
123	  "entity_version": 5
124	}
125	```
126	
127	Response `200` includes updated `version`.
128	
129	### 3.4 Schedule Publish
130	
131	`POST /content/items/{id}/schedule`
132	
133	Request:
134	```json
135	{
136	  "scheduled_at": "ISO-8601",
137	  "entity_version": 3
138	}
139	```
140	
141	Server uses local time and persists a local scheduler job (see `docs/design.md`).
142	
143	### 3.5 Publish (Immutable History Snapshot)
144	
145	`POST /content/items/{id}/publish`
146	
147	Behavior:
148	* On publish, store an immutable snapshot of the content item state for audit.
149	* Publish history stores snapshots (not diffs).
150	
151	Request:
152	```json
153	{
154	  "publish_at": "ISO-8601|null",
155	  "entity_version": 3,
156	  "idempotency_reason": "string|null"
157	}
158	```
159	
160	Response includes:
161	* `published_at`
162	* `publish_history_entry_id`
163	* updated `version`
164	
165	### 3.6 Unpublish
166	
167	`POST /content/items/{id}/unpublish`
168	
169	Creates a new immutable publish history entry capturing the unpublish event and the snapshot at unpublish time.
170	
171	### 3.7 Publish History
172	
173	`GET /content/items/{id}/publish-history`
174	
175	Returns ordered snapshot entries with timestamps.
176	
177	## 4. Comments and Moderation
178	
179	### 4.1 Create Comment (Threaded)
180	
181	`POST /content/items/{contentId}/comments`
182	
183	Request:
184	```json
185	{
186	  "parent_id": "comment_id|null",
187	  "content_text": "string",
188	  "entity_version": 0
189	}
190	```
191	
192	Server-side sensitive-word filtering:
193	* Dictionary is scoped by `organization_id` from the authenticated context.
194	* Matching is exact and case-insensitive variants.
195	
196	Moderation thresholds:
197	* `filter_hit_count == 1` => `moderation_state = "FLAGGED"` (still visible to posting workflow as allowed, but marked for review).
198	* `filter_hit_count >= 2` => `moderation_state = "HOLD"` (held for review).
199	
200	Response `201`:
201	```json
202	{
203	  "comment_id": "id",
204	  "moderation_state": "FLAGGED|HOLD|APPROVED",
205	  "filter_hit_count": 0,
206	  "version": 1
207	}
208	```
209	
210	### 4.2 Query Comments
211	
212	`GET /content/items/{contentId}/comments?state=&threaded=true`
213	
214	Returns moderation-filtered views based on caller role.
215	
216	### 4.3 Moderate Comment
217	
218	`POST /comments/{commentId}/moderate`
219	
220	Request:
221	```json
222	{
223	  "action": "APPROVE|HOLD|REJECT|UNHOLD",
224	  "moderator_notes": "string|null",
225	  "entity_version": 2
226	}
227	```
228	
229	Requires `MODERATOR`.
230	
231	## 5. Sensitive Word Dictionary
232	
233	### 5.1 Add or Update Word
234	
235	`POST /moderation/sensitive-words`
236	
237	Request:
238	```json
239	{
240	  "word": "string",
241	  "replacement": "string|null",
242	  "entity_version": 0
243	}
244	```
245	
246	Dictionary is scoped per `organization_id`.
247	
248	### 5.2 Query Words
249	
250	`GET /moderation/sensitive-words`
251	
252	Requires `MODERATOR` or `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.
253	
254	## 6. Billing and A/R
255	
256	### 6.1 Fee Item Setup
257	
258	`POST /billing/fee-items`
259	
260	Request:
261	```json
262	{
263	  "code": "string",
264	  "calculation_type": "FLAT|PER_UNIT|METERED",
265	  "rate_cents": 1200,
266	  "taxable_flag": true
267	}
268	```
269	
270	### 6.2 Run Billing (Idempotent, Offline-safe)
271	
272	`POST /billing/billing-runs`
273	
274	Request:
275	```json
276	{
277	  "cycle_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
278	  "billing_cycle": "MONTHLY|QUARTERLY",
279	  "idempotency_reason": "string|null",
280	  "requested_by": "string|null"
281	}
282	```
283	
284	Behavior:
285	* Billing generation is scheduled for `12:05 AM` on `cycle_date` using local time.
286	* If billing generation fails at `12:05 AM`, the billing job MUST retry (idempotent) to avoid missing A/R.
287	
288	Response:
289	```json
290	{
291	  "billing_run_id": "id",
292	  "status": "PENDING|RUNNING|SUCCESS|FAILED",
293	  "created_at": "ISO-8601"
294	}
295	```
296	
297	### 6.3 Query Bills
298	
299	`GET /billing/bills?account_id=&cycle_date=&status=&q=`
300	
301	### 6.4 Apply Late Fee (Single Application)
302	
303	Late fee rules:
304	* After 10-day grace period, apply 5% late fee.
305	* Apply once (not continuously).
306	* Cap late fee at $50 per bill.
307	
308	`POST /billing/bills/{billId}/late-fee/apply`
309	
310	Request:
311	```json
312	{
313	  "entity_version": 4,
314	  "applied_for_date": "YYYY-MM-DD|null"
315	}
316	```
317	
318	### 6.5 Discount (Single Discount per Bill)
319	
320	Only one discount may be applied to a single bill.
321	
322	`POST /billing/bills/{billId}/discount`
323	
324	Request:
325	```json
326	{
327	  "discount_type": "PERCENTAGE|FIXED",
328	  "value_cents_or_basis_points": 5000,
329	  "entity_version": 2
330	}
331	```
332	
333	Server validation:
334	* If a discount already exists for the bill and the request would create another conflicting discount, return `409` or `422 BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION`.
335	* Discounts cannot reduce bill below $0.00.
336	
337	## 7. Payments and Settlement
338	
339	Payment methods supported offline:
340	* `CASH|CHECK|VOUCHER|OTHER`
341	
342	### 7.1 Create Settlement (Full or Split)
343	
344	`POST /billing/bills/{billId}/settlements`
345	
346	Request:
347	```json
348	{
349	  "shift_id": "string",
350	  "settlement_mode": "FULL|SPLIT_EVEN|SPLIT_CUSTOM",
351	  "discount_allocation_strategy": "PROPORTIONAL",
352	  "allocations": [
353	    { "payment_method": "CASH|CHECK|VOUCHER|OTHER", "amount_cents": 1234, "payer_seq": 1 }
354	  ],
355	  "entity_version": 3
356	}
357	```
358	
359	Rounding rule (even split):
360	* When splitting evenly produces rounding discrepancies:
361	  * Allocate remainder cents to the first payer (`payer_seq` lowest).
362	
363	Discount allocation:
364	* Allocate discounts proportionally across allocations.
365	
366	Response:
367	```json
368	{
369	  "settlement_id": "id",
370	  "payment_ids": ["..."],
371	  "balance_cents": 0,
372	  "version": 4
373	}
374	```
375	
376	### 7.2 Refund / Reversal (Partial Supported)
377	
378	`POST /billing/payments/{paymentId}/refunds`
379	
380	Request:
381	```json
382	{
383	  "refund_amount_cents": 5000,
384	  "reason": "string|null",
385	  "entity_version": 2
386	}
387	```
388	
389	Behavior:
390	* Partial refunds are allowed.
391	* Refunds are linked to the original payment and validated so totals remain consistent.
392	
393	### 7.3 Ledger and Reconciliation
394	
395	`GET /billing/bills/{billId}/ledger`
396	
397	Used by auditing and discrepancy resolution workflows.
398	
399	### 7.4 End-of-Day Shift Handover and Discrepancies
400	
401	`POST /billing/shifts/{shiftId}/handover`
402	
403	Used by `BILLING_CLERK` to submit end-of-day totals by payment method for a shift.
404	
405	Request:
406	```json
407	{
408	  "totals_by_method": [
409	    { "payment_method": "CASH|CHECK|VOUCHER|OTHER", "total_amount_cents": 12345 }
410	  ],
411	  "entity_version": 0
412	}
413	```
414	
415	Server behavior:
416	* Compare submitted totals against posted A/R for the shift.
417	* If absolute delta > $1.00, create a `discrepancy_case` and:
418	  * assign it to `BILLING_CLERK`
419	  * require `AUDITOR` oversight (read-only review + explicit sign-off)
420	
421	Response:
422	```json
423	{
424	  "handover_id": "id",
425	  "discrepancy_case_id": "id|null",
426	  "status": "RECORDED|DISCREPANCY_OPEN"
427	}
428	```
429	
430	`GET /billing/discrepancies?status=&from=&to=`
431	
432	Requires `BILLING_CLERK` (and `AUDITOR` can view without mutation).
433	
434	`POST /billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve`
435	
436	Request:
437	```json
438	{
439	  "resolution": "APPROVED|REJECTED|NEEDS_MORE_INFO",
440	  "notes": "string|null",
441	  "entity_version": 0
442	}
443	```
444	
445	Requires `AUDITOR` to finalize sign-off.
446	
447	## 8. Dispatch Orders and Driver Eligibility
448	
449	Acceptance constraints:
450	* Max 3 miles (distance computed from driver location vs pickup/drop-off).
451	* Minimum driver rating: 4.2/5.0.
452	* Driver must have at least 15 minutes online today (cumulative).
453	
454	### 8.1 Create Dispatch Order
455	
456	Two modes:
457	
458	* Grab-order (driver selects an available order)
459	* Dispatcher-assigned order (dispatcher assigns a forced dispatch)
460	
461	`POST /dispatch/orders`
462	
463	Request:
464	```json
465	{
466	  "mode": "GRAB|DISPATCHER_ASSIGNED",
467	  "zone_id": "string",
468	  "forced_flag": true,
469	  "rejection_reason_enum": null,
470	  "entity_version": 0
471	}
472	```
473	
474	Zone capacity enforcement:
475	* If zone is at capacity, new orders are queued rather than dropped.
476	
477	Server returns:
478	* `status = QUEUED` with queue position when capacity is reached.
479	
480	### 8.2 Driver Eligibility Check
481	
482	`GET /drivers/{driverId}/eligibility?date=YYYY-MM-DD`
483	
484	Server aggregates driver online session logs to compute cumulative online minutes for the day.
485	
486	### 8.3 Accept Dispatch Order
487	
488	`POST /dispatch/orders/{orderId}/accept`
489	
490	Requires:
491	* eligibility constraints pass
492	* optimistic concurrency version check when changing assignment state
493	
494	### 8.4 Reject Dispatch Order (Forced Dispatch Cooldown)
495	
496	`POST /dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reject`
497	
498	Request:
499	```json
500	{
501	  "reason": "FORCED_DISPATCH_REJECTION_ENUM_VALUE",
502	  "entity_version": 5
503	}
504	```
505	
506	Behavior:
507	* For forced dispatches, after a driver rejects:
508	  * Reassign to another eligible driver.
509	  * Exclude rejecting driver for 30 minutes.
510	
511	### 8.5 Dispatch Order Status
512	
513	`GET /dispatch/orders/{orderId}`
514	
515	## 9. Zone Capacity Queues
516	
517	`GET /dispatch/queues?zone_id=&status=`
518	
519	Dispatcher/clients can monitor queue progression while offline clients reconnect.
520	
521	## 10. Search and Search History Retention
522	
523	### 10.1 Typeahead Search
524	
525	`GET /search/typeahead?q=&filters=...`
526	
527	### 10.2 Record Search History
528	
529	`POST /search/history`
530	
531	Request:
532	```json
533	{
534	  "query": "string",
535	  "filters": { },
536	  "entity_version": 0
537	}
538	```
539	
540	### 10.3 Retention (90 days)
541	
542	Search history retention is enforced by a scheduled cleanup job that deletes records older than 90 days.
543	
544	Endpoint:
545	* `GET /search/history/retention` (read-only config/status; admin/moderator only)
546	
547	## 11. Notifications (Offline Only)
548	
549	No email/SMS/IM network sending is performed by the backend.
550	Channels are configurable but disabled by default and only write to an outbox table.
551	
552	`GET /notifications`
553	
554	`POST /notifications/{notificationId}/ack`
555	
556	## 12. Audit Logs
557	
558	`GET /audit/logs?entity_ref=&from=&to=&action=&q=`
559	
560	## 13. Question-to-API Mapping (1-15)
561	
562	1. Offline-first conflict resolution: optimistic locking via `entity_version` + `409 Conflict` for critical entities.
563	2. Scheduled publishing in offline mode: `POST /content/items/{id}/schedule` uses local-time scheduler with persisted/recovered jobs.
564	3. Sensitive-word filtering scope: sensitive words are scoped by `organization_id` and used during comment creation.
565	4. Comment moderation thresholds: `POST /content/items/{contentId}/comments` sets `FLAGGED` for 1 hit and `HOLD` for 2+ hits.
566	5. Immutable publish history granularity: publish/unpublish creates snapshot-based history entries.
567	6. Billing cycle edge cases: `POST /billing/billing-runs` is idempotent; the `12:05 AM` scheduled run retries on failure.
568	7. Late fee calculation timing: `POST /billing/bills/{billId}/late-fee/apply` applies once and is capped.
569	8. Discount application conflicts: `POST /billing/bills/{billId}/discount` enforces single-discount-per-bill.
570	9. Driver eligibility calculation timing: `GET /drivers/{driverId}/eligibility` uses cumulative online minutes for the day.
571	10. Dispatch rejection handling: `POST /dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reject` enforces 30-minute exclusion and reassignment.
572	11. Zone capacity enforcement: `POST /dispatch/orders` returns `status=QUEUED` when capacity is reached.
573	12. Payment split rounding issues: `POST /billing/bills/{billId}/settlements` allocates remainder cents to the first payer.
574	13. Refund and reversal constraints: `POST /billing/payments/{paymentId}/refunds` supports partial refunds.
575	14. Discrepancy workflow ownership: `POST /billing/shifts/{shiftId}/handover` creates discrepancy cases assigned to Billing Clerk with Auditor sign-off.
576	15. Search history retention enforcement: search history is purged by scheduled deletion older than 90 days (see `GET /search/history/retention`).
577	
578

file.content:
# API Specification

This document defines the REST API surface for the offline-first CivicWorks Community Services backend.
It is written to directly implement the business rules captured in `docs/questions.md` (numbered 1-15).

## 1. Conventions

### Base URL

`/api/v1`

### Authentication and Authorization (RBAC)

* Every request is authenticated and associated with a `user_id` and `role`.
* Access is controlled by role:
  * `SYSTEM_ADMIN`: global configuration and audits.
  * `CONTENT_EDITOR`: create/schedule content.
  * `MODERATOR`: comment review and sensitive-word moderation.
  * `BILLING_CLERK`: fee setup, billing runs, posting and reversals, reconciliation workflows.
  * `DISPATCHER`: zone capacity rules and forced assignments.
  * `DRIVER`: accept/decline work with constraints.
  * `AUDITOR`: read-only financial and activity reporting.

### Idempotency (Offline-safe)

Offline clients may retry requests when the network is unavailable or when connectivity resumes.
The server must treat the same logical operation as a single action.

* For all mutating requests (`POST`, `PATCH`, `PUT`, `DELETE`), clients MUST send:
  * `Idempotency-Key: <uuid>`
* Server behavior:
  * If the same key is seen again for the same authenticated user, return the original result (HTTP 200/201/204/409 as appropriate).
  * Idempotency keys MUST be persisted locally (PostgreSQL) so retries remain correct across restarts.

### Optimistic Concurrency Control (Conflict Detection)

To resolve offline-first conflicts safely, the API uses optimistic locking for critical entities.

* Entities that support conflict detection include: `ContentItem`, `Comment`, `Bill`, `Payment`, `DispatchOrder`.
* For updates/mutations that change state:
  * Client sends `entity_version` in the request body.
  * Server compares to current `version` stored in the database.
  * On mismatch:
    * Return `409 Conflict` with a payload describing the server’s current state and a suggested reconciliation path.

### Money Types

* Use integer cents everywhere in requests/responses:
  * `amount_cents`, `balance_cents`, `fee_cents`, `discount_cents`.
* When the UI operates with decimals, convert to cents client-side to avoid floating errors.

## 2. Error Model

All errors return JSON:

```json
{
  "error_code": "VALIDATION_FAILED",
  "message": "Human readable summary",
  "details": { }
}
```

Common `error_code` values:

* `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`
* `VALIDATION_FAILED`
* `CONFLICT_VERSION_MISMATCH`
* `IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT`
* `BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION`
* `SCHEDULE_NOT_ALLOWED`

## 3. Content and Resource Management

Rich-text is stored as sanitized HTML. The server must sanitize and store the final HTML.

### 3.1 Create Content Item

`POST /content/items`

Request:

```json
{
  "type": "NEWS|POLICY|EVENT|CLASS",
  "title": "string",
  "sanitized_body_html": "<html>...</html>",
  "tags": ["string"],
  "scheduled_at": "ISO-8601|null",
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Response `201`:

```json
{
  "id": "content_item_id",
  "state": "DRAFT|SCHEDULED|PUBLISHED",
  "published_at": "ISO-8601|null",
  "version": 1
}
```

### 3.2 Query Content Items

`GET /content/items?type=&tag=&state=&from=&to=&q=`

Supports filtering and full-text search (server-side).

### 3.3 Update Content Item

`PATCH /content/items/{id}`

Request:

```json
{
  "title": "string|null",
  "sanitized_body_html": "<html>...</html>|null",
  "tags": ["string"]|null,
  "scheduled_at": "ISO-8601|null",
  "entity_version": 5
}
```

Response `200` includes updated `version`.

### 3.4 Schedule Publish

`POST /content/items/{id}/schedule`

Request:
```json
{
  "scheduled_at": "ISO-8601",
  "entity_version": 3
}
```

Server uses local time and persists a local scheduler job (see `docs/design.md`).

### 3.5 Publish (Immutable History Snapshot)

`POST /content/items/{id}/publish`

Behavior:
* On publish, store an immutable snapshot of the content item state for audit.
* Publish history stores snapshots (not diffs).

Request:
```json
{
  "publish_at": "ISO-8601|null",
  "entity_version": 3,
  "idempotency_reason": "string|null"
}
```

Response includes:
* `published_at`
* `publish_history_entry_id`
* updated `version`

### 3.6 Unpublish

`POST /content/items/{id}/unpublish`

Creates a new immutable publish history entry capturing the unpublish event and the snapshot at unpublish time.

### 3.7 Publish History

`GET /content/items/{id}/publish-history`

Returns ordered snapshot entries with timestamps.

## 4. Comments and Moderation

### 4.1 Create Comment (Threaded)

`POST /content/items/{contentId}/comments`

Request:
```json
{
  "parent_id": "comment_id|null",
  "content_text": "string",
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Server-side sensitive-word filtering:
* Dictionary is scoped by `organization_id` from the authenticated context.
* Matching is exact and case-insensitive variants.

Moderation thresholds:
* `filter_hit_count == 1` => `moderation_state = "FLAGGED"` (still visible to posting workflow as allowed, but marked for review).
* `filter_hit_count >= 2` => `moderation_state = "HOLD"` (held for review).

Response `201`:
```json
{
  "comment_id": "id",
  "moderation_state": "FLAGGED|HOLD|APPROVED",
  "filter_hit_count": 0,
  "version": 1
}
```

### 4.2 Query Comments

`GET /content/items/{contentId}/comments?state=&threaded=true`

Returns moderation-filtered views based on caller role.

### 4.3 Moderate Comment

`POST /comments/{commentId}/moderate`

Request:
```json
{
  "action": "APPROVE|HOLD|REJECT|UNHOLD",
  "moderator_notes": "string|null",
  "entity_version": 2
}
```

Requires `MODERATOR`.

## 5. Sensitive Word Dictionary

### 5.1 Add or Update Word

`POST /moderation/sensitive-words`

Request:
```json
{
  "word": "string",
  "replacement": "string|null",
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Dictionary is scoped per `organization_id`.

### 5.2 Query Words

`GET /moderation/sensitive-words`

Requires `MODERATOR` or `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.

## 6. Billing and A/R

### 6.1 Fee Item Setup

`POST /billing/fee-items`

Request:
```json
{
  "code": "string",
  "calculation_type": "FLAT|PER_UNIT|METERED",
  "rate_cents": 1200,
  "taxable_flag": true
}
```

### 6.2 Run Billing (Idempotent, Offline-safe)

`POST /billing/billing-runs`

Request:
```json
{
  "cycle_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "billing_cycle": "MONTHLY|QUARTERLY",
  "idempotency_reason": "string|null",
  "requested_by": "string|null"
}
```

Behavior:
* Billing generation is scheduled for `12:05 AM` on `cycle_date` using local time.
* If billing generation fails at `12:05 AM`, the billing job MUST retry (idempotent) to avoid missing A/R.

Response:
```json
{
  "billing_run_id": "id",
  "status": "PENDING|RUNNING|SUCCESS|FAILED",
  "created_at": "ISO-8601"
}
```

### 6.3 Query Bills

`GET /billing/bills?account_id=&cycle_date=&status=&q=`

### 6.4 Apply Late Fee (Single Application)

Late fee rules:
* After 10-day grace period, apply 5% late fee.
* Apply once (not continuously).
* Cap late fee at $50 per bill.

`POST /billing/bills/{billId}/late-fee/apply`

Request:
```json
{
  "entity_version": 4,
  "applied_for_date": "YYYY-MM-DD|null"
}
```

### 6.5 Discount (Single Discount per Bill)

Only one discount may be applied to a single bill.

`POST /billing/bills/{billId}/discount`

Request:
```json
{
  "discount_type": "PERCENTAGE|FIXED",
  "value_cents_or_basis_points": 5000,
  "entity_version": 2
}
```

Server validation:
* If a discount already exists for the bill and the request would create another conflicting discount, return `409` or `422 BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION`.
* Discounts cannot reduce bill below $0.00.

## 7. Payments and Settlement

Payment methods supported offline:
* `CASH|CHECK|VOUCHER|OTHER`

### 7.1 Create Settlement (Full or Split)

`POST /billing/bills/{billId}/settlements`

Request:
```json
{
  "shift_id": "string",
  "settlement_mode": "FULL|SPLIT_EVEN|SPLIT_CUSTOM",
  "discount_allocation_strategy": "PROPORTIONAL",
  "allocations": [
    { "payment_method": "CASH|CHECK|VOUCHER|OTHER", "amount_cents": 1234, "payer_seq": 1 }
  ],
  "entity_version": 3
}
```

Rounding rule (even split):
* When splitting evenly produces rounding discrepancies:
  * Allocate remainder cents to the first payer (`payer_seq` lowest).

Discount allocation:
* Allocate discounts proportionally across allocations.

Response:
```json
{
  "settlement_id": "id",
  "payment_ids": ["..."],
  "balance_cents": 0,
  "version": 4
}
```

### 7.2 Refund / Reversal (Partial Supported)

`POST /billing/payments/{paymentId}/refunds`

Request:
```json
{
  "refund_amount_cents": 5000,
  "reason": "string|null",
  "entity_version": 2
}
```

Behavior:
* Partial refunds are allowed.
* Refunds are linked to the original payment and validated so totals remain consistent.

### 7.3 Ledger and Reconciliation

`GET /billing/bills/{billId}/ledger`

Used by auditing and discrepancy resolution workflows.

### 7.4 End-of-Day Shift Handover and Discrepancies

`POST /billing/shifts/{shiftId}/handover`

Used by `BILLING_CLERK` to submit end-of-day totals by payment method for a shift.

Request:
```json
{
  "totals_by_method": [
    { "payment_method": "CASH|CHECK|VOUCHER|OTHER", "total_amount_cents": 12345 }
  ],
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Server behavior:
* Compare submitted totals against posted A/R for the shift.
* If absolute delta > $1.00, create a `discrepancy_case` and:
  * assign it to `BILLING_CLERK`
  * require `AUDITOR` oversight (read-only review + explicit sign-off)

Response:
```json
{
  "handover_id": "id",
  "discrepancy_case_id": "id|null",
  "status": "RECORDED|DISCREPANCY_OPEN"
}
```

`GET /billing/discrepancies?status=&from=&to=`

Requires `BILLING_CLERK` (and `AUDITOR` can view without mutation).

`POST /billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve`

Request:
```json
{
  "resolution": "APPROVED|REJECTED|NEEDS_MORE_INFO",
  "notes": "string|null",
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Requires `AUDITOR` to finalize sign-off.

## 8. Dispatch Orders and Driver Eligibility

Acceptance constraints:
* Max 3 miles (distance computed from driver location vs pickup/drop-off).
* Minimum driver rating: 4.2/5.0.
* Driver must have at least 15 minutes online today (cumulative).

### 8.1 Create Dispatch Order

Two modes:

* Grab-order (driver selects an available order)
* Dispatcher-assigned order (dispatcher assigns a forced dispatch)

`POST /dispatch/orders`

Request:
```json
{
  "mode": "GRAB|DISPATCHER_ASSIGNED",
  "zone_id": "string",
  "forced_flag": true,
  "rejection_reason_enum": null,
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Zone capacity enforcement:
* If zone is at capacity, new orders are queued rather than dropped.

Server returns:
* `status = QUEUED` with queue position when capacity is reached.

### 8.2 Driver Eligibility Check

`GET /drivers/{driverId}/eligibility?date=YYYY-MM-DD`

Server aggregates driver online session logs to compute cumulative online minutes for the day.

### 8.3 Accept Dispatch Order

`POST /dispatch/orders/{orderId}/accept`

Requires:
* eligibility constraints pass
* optimistic concurrency version check when changing assignment state

### 8.4 Reject Dispatch Order (Forced Dispatch Cooldown)

`POST /dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reject`

Request:
```json
{
  "reason": "FORCED_DISPATCH_REJECTION_ENUM_VALUE",
  "entity_version": 5
}
```

Behavior:
* For forced dispatches, after a driver rejects:
  * Reassign to another eligible driver.
  * Exclude rejecting driver for 30 minutes.

### 8.5 Dispatch Order Status

`GET /dispatch/orders/{orderId}`

## 9. Zone Capacity Queues

`GET /dispatch/queues?zone_id=&status=`

Dispatcher/clients can monitor queue progression while offline clients reconnect.

## 10. Search and Search History Retention

### 10.1 Typeahead Search

`GET /search/typeahead?q=&filters=...`

### 10.2 Record Search History

`POST /search/history`

Request:
```json
{
  "query": "string",
  "filters": { },
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

### 10.3 Retention (90 days)

Search history retention is enforced by a scheduled cleanup job that deletes records older than 90 days.

Endpoint:
* `GET /search/history/retention` (read-only config/status; admin/moderator only)

## 11. Notifications (Offline Only)

No email/SMS/IM network sending is performed by the backend.
Channels are configurable but disabled by default and only write to an outbox table.

`GET /notifications`

`POST /notifications/{notificationId}/ack`

## 12. Audit Logs

`GET /audit/logs?entity_ref=&from=&to=&action=&q=`

## 13. Question-to-API Mapping (1-15)

1. Offline-first conflict resolution: optimistic locking via `entity_version` + `409 Conflict` for critical entities.
2. Scheduled publishing in offline mode: `POST /content/items/{id}/schedule` uses local-time scheduler with persisted/recovered jobs.
3. Sensitive-word filtering scope: sensitive words are scoped by `organization_id` and used during comment creation.
4. Comment moderation thresholds: `POST /content/items/{contentId}/comments` sets `FLAGGED` for 1 hit and `HOLD` for 2+ hits.
5. Immutable publish history granularity: publish/unpublish creates snapshot-based history entries.
6. Billing cycle edge cases: `POST /billing/billing-runs` is idempotent; the `12:05 AM` scheduled run retries on failure.
7. Late fee calculation timing: `POST /billing/bills/{billId}/late-fee/apply` applies once and is capped.
8. Discount application conflicts: `POST /billing/bills/{billId}/discount` enforces single-discount-per-bill.
9. Driver eligibility calculation timing: `GET /drivers/{driverId}/eligibility` uses cumulative online minutes for the day.
10. Dispatch rejection handling: `POST /dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reject` enforces 30-minute exclusion and reassignment.
11. Zone capacity enforcement: `POST /dispatch/orders` returns `status=QUEUED` when capacity is reached.
12. Payment split rounding issues: `POST /billing/bills/{billId}/settlements` allocates remainder cents to the first payer.
13. Refund and reversal constraints: `POST /billing/payments/{paymentId}/refunds` supports partial refunds.
14. Discrepancy workflow ownership: `POST /billing/shifts/{shiftId}/handover` creates discrepancy cases assigned to Billing Clerk with Auditor sign-off.
15. Search history retention enforcement: search history is purged by scheduled deletion older than 90 days (see `GET /search/history/retention`).

content.content:
1	1. Verdict
2	- Fail
3	
4	2. Scope and Verification Boundary
5	- Reviewed: business prompt (`docs/prompt.md`), delivery docs (`repo/README.md`), runtime/test scripts (`repo/run_tests.ps1`, `repo/run_tests.sh`), security and domain implementation across controllers/services/repositories/entities/migrations, and test suites under `repo/src/test/java`, `repo/unit_tests/src/test/java`, and `repo/API_tests/tests`.
6	- Not executed: runtime startup and end-to-end API verification were not executed.
7	- Docker-based verification required but not executed: yes. Documented startup/test flows are Docker-based (`repo/README.md:8`, `repo/run_tests.ps1:30-38`, `repo/run_tests.sh:41-50`), and Docker commands were intentionally not run per review constraints.
8	- Remains unconfirmed: live runtime behavior, migration success on a fresh DB, and actual pass/fail status of unit/API tests in this environment.
9	
10	3. Top Findings
11	
12	1) Severity: High
13	- Conclusion: `AUDITOR` can access globally scoped audit logs without tenant filtering.
14	- Brief rationale: Reporting endpoints should not bypass tenant boundaries when organization scoping is used across core business domains.
15	- Evidence:
16	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/AuditController.java:33-45` exposes `/api/v1/audit/logs` to `AUDITOR` and calls unscoped service queries.
17	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/AuditService.java:60-69` has no actor/org scoping in `findAll`/`findWithFilters`.
18	  - `src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql:326-333` defines `audit_logs` without `organization_id`.
19	- Impact: In multi-organization deployments, auditors can read cross-organization activity/financial traces.
20	- Minimum actionable fix: Add `organization_id` to audit logs, populate it at write time, and enforce org-scoped reads for `AUDITOR` (retain global visibility only for `SYSTEM_ADMIN`).
21	
22	2) Severity: High
23	- Conclusion: KPI report endpoint returns all organizations' reports to `AUDITOR`.
24	- Brief rationale: Financial reporting data is tenant-sensitive and should be scoped unless explicitly global-admin only.
25	- Evidence:
26	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/KpiReportController.java:27-30` returns `findLatestPerOrganization()` for both `AUDITOR` and `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.
27	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/repository/KpiReportRepository.java:20-28` query explicitly returns latest report for every organization.
28	- Impact: Cross-tenant financial exposure risk for non-admin auditors.
29	- Minimum actionable fix: Add org-scoped KPI query for non-admin users and gate global query to `SYSTEM_ADMIN` only.
30	
31	3) Severity: High
32	- Conclusion: Dispatch auxiliary endpoints lack object-level/tenant authorization checks.
33	- Brief rationale: `DRIVER` can query eligibility for arbitrary `driverId`; queue reads are zone-only without org checks.
34	- Evidence:
35	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/DispatchController.java:121-131` allows `DRIVER`/`DISPATCHER` access to `/dispatch/queues` and `/drivers/{driverId}/eligibility` without actor-aware scoping.
36	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/DispatchService.java:319-323` loads any driver by ID in `checkDriverEligibility` (no actor/org ownership check).
37	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/DispatchService.java:397-399` returns queues by `zoneId` only.
38	  - `src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql:232-239` and `src/main/java/com/civicworks/domain/entity/ZoneQueue.java:18-23` show queue records have no org discriminator.
39	- Impact: Driver data enumeration and possible cross-organization queue/order metadata leakage.
40	- Minimum actionable fix: Enforce self-only eligibility access for `DRIVER`, org-scoped eligibility for `DISPATCHER`, and org-scoped queue reads (schema + query changes where needed).
41	
42	4) Severity: Medium
43	- Conclusion: Delivery documentation contradicts implemented route authorization for discrepancy resolution.
44	- Brief rationale: Acceptance gate 1.1 requires runtime behavior to align with delivery docs.
45	- Evidence:
46	  - `repo/README.md:55` documents `POST /api/v1/billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve` as `(AUDITOR)`.
47	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/BillingController.java:198-200` enforces `BILLING_CLERK` or `SYSTEM_ADMIN` only.
48	- Impact: Operators/testers following README will hit unexpected 403 behavior and mis-validate role boundaries.
49	- Minimum actionable fix: Update README role mapping (or code) and keep endpoint authorization docs in sync with `@PreAuthorize`.
50	
51	5) Severity: Medium
52	- Conclusion: Idempotency key scope is user+key only; action type is not part of uniqueness/replay lookup.
53	- Brief rationale: Reusing one key across different mutation types can replay an unrelated cached response for the same user.
54	- Evidence:
55	  - `src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql:347` unique constraint is `UNIQUE(user_id, idempotency_key)`.
56	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/repository/IdempotencyRecordRepository.java:13` lookup is by user+key only.
57	  - `src/main/java/com/civicworks/config/IdempotencyGuard.java:68` replays cached response from that lookup; `actionType` is not part of retrieval key.
58	  - Design intent in `docs/api-spec.md:38-39` expects `(user_id, idempotency_key, action_type)`.
59	- Impact: Data integrity/API correctness risk under key reuse across endpoints.
60	- Minimum actionable fix: Include `action_type` in DB uniqueness and repository lookup; reject cross-action reuse with explicit conflict handling.
61	
62	4. Security Summary
63	- authentication: Partial Pass
64	  - Evidence: Global authentication enforced (`src/main/java/com/civicworks/config/SecurityConfig.java:39-43`), but account `status` is not evaluated during user loading (`src/main/java/com/civicworks/config/SecurityConfig.java:51-57`).
65	- route authorization: Partial Pass
66	  - Evidence: Most mutating/read endpoints use role guards (`@PreAuthorize`) across controllers, but dispatch auxiliary routes are over-permissive for `DRIVER` (`src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/DispatchController.java:121-131`).
67	- object-level authorization: Partial Pass
68	  - Evidence: Strong checks exist in core mutation services (e.g., billing/content/notification ownership), but missing actor-bound checks in eligibility/queue/reporting endpoints (see Findings 1-3).
69	- tenant / user isolation: Fail
70	  - Evidence: Unscoped audit/KPI visibility and dispatch auxiliary endpoints can expose data outside least-privilege boundaries (Findings 1-3).
71	
72	5. Test Sufficiency Summary
73	- Test Overview
74	  - Unit tests exist: yes (`repo/src/test/java/...`, `repo/unit_tests/src/test/java/...`).
75	  - API / integration tests exist: yes (`repo/API_tests/tests/...`).
76	  - Obvious test entry points: `repo/run_tests.ps1`, `repo/run_tests.sh`, plus Maven tests in `repo/pom.xml`.
77	- Core Coverage
78	  - happy path: partially covered
79	    - Evidence: Content/billing/dispatch happy-path tests are present (e.g., `repo/API_tests/tests/test_content.py`, `repo/API_tests/tests/test_billing.py`, `repo/API_tests/tests/test_dispatch.py`).
80	  - key failure paths: partially covered
81	    - Evidence: Validation/version/idempotency failure tests exist (e.g., `repo/API_tests/tests/test_idempotency.py`, `repo/src/test/java/com/civicworks/version/...`).
82	  - security-critical coverage: partially covered
83	    - Evidence: Cross-tenant billing/content and forced-dispatch hijack tests exist (`repo/unit_tests/src/test/java/com/civicworks/unit/CrossTenantBillingTest.java`, `repo/unit_tests/src/test/java/com/civicworks/unit/CrossTenantContentTest.java`, `repo/unit_tests/src/test/java/com/civicworks/unit/ForcedDispatchHijackTest.java`).
84	- Major Gaps
85	  - Missing authorization/tenant tests for `/api/v1/audit/logs` scoping by organization.
86	  - Missing authorization/tenant tests for `/api/v1/reports/kpi` scoping by organization.
87	  - Missing negative tests enforcing that `DRIVER` cannot query other drivers' eligibility or foreign zone queues.
88	- Final Test Verdict
89	  - Partial Pass (broad test inventory exists, but key security boundaries above are not sufficiently covered and tests were not executed in this review run).
90	
91	6. Engineering Quality Summary
92	- The project is structurally credible for a 0-to-1 backend: clear module decomposition (controllers/services/repositories), Flyway migrations, scheduler jobs, and baseline documentation.
93	- Major delivery-confidence issue is inconsistent authorization architecture: core business mutations are heavily hardened, while reporting/auxiliary endpoints bypass equivalent tenant/object isolation.
94	- Documentation drift and idempotency-key model drift increase operational and integration risk despite otherwise professional implementation patterns.
95	
96	7. Next Actions
97	- 1) Fix tenant scoping for audit and KPI endpoints (and schema where required), then add regression tests.
98	- 2) Lock down dispatch auxiliary endpoints: self-only eligibility for drivers, org-scoped reads for dispatchers/admins, and queue org isolation.
99	- 3) Align `repo/README.md` role documentation with actual `@PreAuthorize` rules (especially discrepancy resolution).
100	- 4) Upgrade idempotency model to `(user_id, idempotency_key, action_type)` and add collision tests across different mutation endpoints.
101	- 5) Re-run full documented verification (`docker compose up --build`, then `run_tests`) and attach concrete runtime evidence.
102

  - Missing authorization/tenant tests for `/api/v1/reports/kpi` scoping by organization.
  - Missing negative tests enforcing that `DRIVER` cannot query other drivers' eligibility or foreign zone queues.
- Final Test Verdict
  - Partial Pass (broad test inventory exists, but key security boundaries above are not sufficiently covered and tests were not executed in this review run).

6. Engineering Quality Summary
- The project is structurally credible for a 0-to-1 backend: clear module decomposition (controllers/services/repositories), Flyway migrations, scheduler jobs, and baseline documentation.
- Major delivery-confidence issue is inconsistent authorization architecture: core business mutations are heavily hardened, while reporting/auxiliary endpoints bypass equivalent tenant/object isolation.
- Documentation drift and idempotency-key model drift increase operational and integration risk despite otherwise professional implementation patterns.

7. Next Actions
- 1) Fix tenant scoping for audit and KPI endpoints (and schema where required), then add regression tests.
- 2) Lock down dispatch auxiliary endpoints: self-only eligibility for drivers, org-scoped reads for dispatchers/admins, and queue org isolation.
- 3) Align `repo/README.md` role documentation with actual `@PreAuthorize` rules (especially discrepancy resolution).
- 4) Upgrade idempotency model to `(user_id, idempotency_key, action_type)` and add collision tests across different mutation endpoints.
- 5) Re-run full documented verification (`docker compose up --build`, then `run_tests`) and attach concrete runtime evidence.

file.content:
1. Verdict
- Fail

2. Scope and Verification Boundary
- Reviewed: prompt/spec/design docs (`docs/prompt.md`, `docs/api-spec.md`, `docs/design.md`), delivery docs (`repo/README.md`), security-sensitive code paths (auth, reporting, dispatch, idempotency), migrations, and test suites under `repo/src/test/java`, `repo/unit_tests/src/test/java`, and `repo/API_tests/tests`.
- Executed: `mvn -B test` in `repo` (result: `Tests run: 250, Failures: 0, Errors: 0`, `BUILD SUCCESS`).
- Not executed: application startup and API end-to-end flow via Docker were not run.
- Docker-based verification required but not executed: yes (documented startup/test paths are Docker-based in `repo/README.md:8`, `repo/run_tests.ps1:30-38`, `repo/run_tests.sh:41-50`).
- Remains unconfirmed: runtime behavior in containerized startup path (`docker compose up --build`) and API pytest execution in Docker.

3. Top Findings

1) Severity: High
- Conclusion: Audit log read path still has no tenant isolation for `AUDITOR`.
- Brief rationale: The endpoint and service query all audit records, while the audit schema has no organization discriminator.
- Evidence:
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/AuditController.java:33-45` calls global `findAll` / `findWithFilters` without actor/org scope.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/AuditService.java:60-69` exposes unscoped reads.
  - `repo/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql:326-333` defines `audit_logs` without `organization_id`.
- Impact: Cross-organization activity traces can be viewed by non-admin auditors.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add `organization_id` to `audit_logs`, populate at write-time, and enforce org-scoped reads for `AUDITOR` while preserving global access for `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.

2) Severity: High
- Conclusion: KPI reporting endpoint remains globally readable by `AUDITOR`.
- Brief rationale: Financial reporting is tenant-sensitive; current query returns latest report for every organization.
- Evidence:
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/KpiReportController.java:27-30` returns `findLatestPerOrganization()` for both `AUDITOR` and `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/repository/KpiReportRepository.java:20-28` query is explicitly global across organizations.
- Impact: Cross-tenant financial information disclosure.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add org-scoped KPI retrieval for non-admin users and restrict global list behavior to `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.

3) Severity: High
- Conclusion: Dispatch auxiliary endpoints still miss actor-bound/object-bound authorization.
- Brief rationale: A `DRIVER` can query arbitrary `driverId` eligibility; queue access is zone-only and not org-scoped.
- Evidence:
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/DispatchController.java:121-131` exposes `/dispatch/queues` and `/drivers/{driverId}/eligibility` without actor context checks.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/DispatchService.java:319-323` loads driver eligibility by raw `driverId` only.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/DispatchService.java:397-399` returns queue by `zoneId` only.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/domain/entity/ZoneQueue.java:18-23` shows queue entries have no org discriminator.
- Impact: Cross-user driver data enumeration and possible cross-tenant queue visibility.
- Minimum actionable fix: Enforce self-only eligibility for `DRIVER`, org-scoped checks for `DISPATCHER`, and org-scoped queue reads (including schema/query support where needed).

4) Severity: Medium
- Conclusion: Idempotency still replays by `(user_id, idempotency_key)` and ignores `action_type` in lookup/uniqueness.
- Brief rationale: Reusing the same key across different mutation actions can replay the wrong cached result.
- Evidence:
  - `repo/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__initial_schema.sql:347` keeps `UNIQUE(user_id, idempotency_key)`.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/repository/IdempotencyRecordRepository.java:13` lookup is `findByUserIdAndIdempotencyKey(...)`.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/config/IdempotencyGuard.java:68-79` replays cached response from that lookup regardless of `actionType`.
  - Design contract expects action-type keying: `docs/design.md:38`.
- Impact: Data integrity/API correctness risk when clients reuse keys across endpoints.
- Minimum actionable fix: Change persistence and lookup to `(user_id, idempotency_key, action_type)` and return explicit conflict on cross-action key reuse.

5) Severity: Medium
- Conclusion: README authorization mapping remains inconsistent with implemented discrepancy resolution authorization.
- Brief rationale: Delivery documentation should match actual runtime access control.
- Evidence:
  - `repo/README.md:55` states discrepancy resolve endpoint is `(AUDITOR)`.
  - `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/BillingController.java:198-200` allows only `BILLING_CLERK` or `SYSTEM_ADMIN`.
- Impact: Operator/test confusion and false-positive acceptance checks.
- Minimum actionable fix: Update README role mapping (or code policy) so docs and enforcement match.

4. Security Summary
- authentication: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: Global authentication is enforced (`repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/config/SecurityConfig.java:39-43`), but user account `status` exists (`repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/domain/entity/User.java:34-36`) and is not checked during authentication (`repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/config/SecurityConfig.java:51-57`).
- route authorization: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: Most endpoints use role checks, but high-impact reporting/dispatch auxiliary routes remain over-broad (Findings 1-3).
- object-level authorization: Fail
  - Evidence: Missing actor/object binding on driver eligibility and queue reads (`repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/controller/DispatchController.java:121-131`, `repo/src/main/java/com/civicworks/service/DispatchService.java:319-323`).
- tenant / user isolation: Fail
  - Evidence: Audit/KPI global reads and dispatch auxiliary path leakage remain unresolved (Findings 1-3).

5. Test Sufficiency Summary
- Test Overview
  - whether unit tests exist: Yes (`repo/src/test/java/...` and `repo/unit_tests/src/test/java/...`).
  - whether API / integration tests exist: Yes (`repo/API_tests/tests/...`).
  - obvious test entry points if present: `mvn -B test`, plus Docker-driven scripts `repo/run_tests.ps1` and `repo/run_tests.sh`.
- Core Coverage
  - happy path: covered
    - Evidence: `mvn -B test` passed with 250 tests; includes content/billing/dispatch happy-path suites.
  - key failure paths: covered
    - Evidence: version/idempotency/validation and conflict tests are present and passing (`repo/src/test/java/com/civicworks/version/...`, `repo/src/test/java/com/civicworks/idempotency/...`).
  - security-critical coverage: partial
    - Evidence: Strong tests exist for cross-tenant billing/content and forced-dispatch hijack; but no tests cover audit/KPI tenant scoping and no negative self-vs-other driver eligibility checks.
- Major Gaps
  - Missing audit tenant-scoping tests for `/api/v1/audit/logs` (existing coverage only confirms role access in `repo/API_tests/tests/test_permissions.py:109`).
  - Missing KPI tenant-scoping tests for `/api/v1/reports/kpi` (no endpoint test usage found in `repo/API_tests/tests`).
  - Missing negative authorization tests for `DRIVER` querying other drivers’ eligibility and for `/api/v1/dispatch/queues` tenant isolation.
- Final Test Verdict
  - Partial Pass

6. Engineering Quality Summary
- Core architecture remains strong (modular controllers/services/repositories, migrations, scheduler, structured exception handling, substantial automated tests).
- Delivery confidence is still materially reduced by unresolved authorization and tenant-isolation gaps in reporting and dispatch auxiliary APIs.
- Professionalism gap remains in doc-to-implementation drift and idempotency contract drift from design intent.

7. Next Actions
- 1) Implement tenant-scoped audit logging end-to-end (schema + write path + read filters), then add regression tests.
- 2) Restrict KPI endpoint by role and organization (auditor scoped, admin global), then add endpoint tests.
- 3) Enforce self/org checks on eligibility and queue endpoints; add negative authorization tests.
- 4) Upgrade idempotency uniqueness/lookup to include `action_type` and add cross-action collision tests.
- 5) Align `repo/README.md` authorization descriptions with `@PreAuthorize` rules.

# CivicWorks Community Services Backend

Offline-first municipal community services platform.

## Quick Start

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

The application is published on the host at **http://localhost:18080** by default (container still listens on 8080 inside the network). Another process on your machine may already use 8080; this mapping avoids that conflict. To use host port 8080 instead, run: `HOST_APP_PORT=8080 docker compose up` (PowerShell: `$env:HOST_APP_PORT=8080; docker compose up`).

The PostgreSQL database runs on host port **5432**.

## Default Credentials

All users have password `admin123`.

| Username   | Role           |
|------------|----------------|
| admin      | SYSTEM_ADMIN   |
| editor     | CONTENT_EDITOR |
| clerk      | BILLING_CLERK  |
| driver1    | DRIVER         |
| moderator  | MODERATOR      |
| dispatcher | DISPATCHER     |
| auditor    | AUDITOR        |

## API Base URL

`http://localhost:18080/api/v1` (or your `HOST_APP_PORT` if set)

## Authentication

HTTP Basic Auth on every request.

## Key Endpoints

### Content Management
- POST /api/v1/content/items - Create content item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- GET /api/v1/content/items - List content items (authenticated)
- PATCH /api/v1/content/items/{id} - Update content item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/content/items/{id}/publish - Publish item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/content/items/{id}/unpublish - Unpublish item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN, MODERATOR)
- GET /api/v1/content/items/{id}/publish-history - Publish history (authenticated)

### Billing
- POST /api/v1/billing/fee-items - Create fee item (BILLING_CLERK, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/billing/billing-runs - Create billing run (BILLING_CLERK, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/billing/bills/{billId}/late-fee/apply - Apply late fee (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/bills/{billId}/discount - Apply discount (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/bills/{billId}/settlements - Create settlement (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/payments/{paymentId}/refunds - Issue refund (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/shifts/{shiftId}/handover - Submit shift handover (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve - Resolve discrepancy (AUDITOR)

### Dispatch
- POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders - Create order (DISPATCHER, DRIVER)
- POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/accept - Accept order (DRIVER)
- POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reject - Reject order (DRIVER)
- GET /api/v1/drivers/{driverId}/eligibility - Driver eligibility (DISPATCHER, DRIVER)

#### Create order — required fields

`pickupLat` and `pickupLng` are **required** at order creation.  Orders without a
pickup location cannot be accepted (the 3-mile distance check requires a trusted
server-side coordinate source).

```json
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders
{
  "zoneId":     "<uuid>",
  "mode":       "GRAB",
  "pickupLat":  37.77,
  "pickupLng":  -122.41,
  "dropoffLat": 37.78,
  "dropoffLng": -122.42
}
```

#### Accept order — required request body

`driverLat` and `driverLng` are **required** in the request body.  The server
performs a Haversine distance check and rejects the acceptance (HTTP 400) if
either the driver coordinates or the order pickup coordinates are missing, or if
the driver is more than 3 miles from the pickup location.

```
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/accept
Headers:
  X-Entity-Version: <version>          (required — optimistic locking)
  Idempotency-Key:  <uuid>             (required)
Body:
{
  "driverLat": 37.77,
  "driverLng": -122.41
}
```

### Search
- GET /api/v1/search/typeahead?q={query} - Full-text search (authenticated)

### Audit
- GET /api/v1/audit/logs - Audit logs (AUDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)

## Backups

A `backup` service runs alongside the application in `docker-compose.yml`.
Every 24 hours it calls `pg_dump` against the `db` service and writes a
gzip-compressed snapshot to the `civicworks_backups` named Docker volume:

```
/backups/civicworks_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.sql.gz
```

**Retention policy:** backup files older than 7 days are automatically deleted
by the same container process (`find … -mtime +7 -delete`), so the volume
never accumulates more than ~8 snapshots under normal operation.

To inspect or restore a backup, attach to the volume or copy a file out:

```bash
# List available snapshots
docker run --rm -v civicworks_backups:/backups alpine ls /backups

# Restore a specific snapshot (replace <file> with the desired filename)
docker run --rm -v civicworks_backups:/backups postgres:16-alpine \
  sh -c "gunzip -c /backups/<file> | psql -h db -U civicworks civicworks"
```

## Final Fixes (Acceptance Blockers)

The following changes were made to resolve final acceptance blockers:

| Blocker | File(s) changed | Why |
|---------|----------------|-----|
| Flyway duplicate DDL | `V1__initial_schema.sql`, `V3__extensions.sql` | V1 and V3 both defined `notification_outbox`, causing Flyway to fail on every fresh DB. V1's definition removed; V3 is the single source of truth with `IF NOT EXISTS` guard. |
| Dispatch bypass via missing pickup coords | `CreateDispatchOrderRequest.java`, `DispatchService.java` | `pickupLat`/`pickupLng` are now `@NotNull` at order creation. `acceptOrder` rejects with 400 if the order has no pickup location, closing the last bypass path. |
| API test contract drift | `API_tests/tests/test_dispatch.py` | Accept-order tests updated to include required `driverLat`/`driverLng` body; new negative test asserts 400 for missing driver coords; new test asserts 422 for missing pickup coords at order creation. |
| SYSTEM_ADMIN global billing scope | `BillingController.java` | Replaced `actor.getOrganization() != null ? orgId : null` with an explicit role check: `SYSTEM_ADMIN` always passes `null` orgId (global), regardless of whether the account has an org set. |
| Template-driven reminder notifications | `NotificationTemplateService.java` (new), `ReminderNotificationJob.java` | Hardcoded subject/body strings in the scheduler replaced with a `{{placeholder}}`-based template engine. `OVERDUE_BILL_REMINDER` is a built-in template; new templates can be registered at runtime. |

## Security and Integrity Fixes

| Fix | File(s) changed | Why |
|-----|----------------|-----|
| Billing tenant isolation | `BillingService.java`, `PaymentService.java` | `applyLateFee`, `applyDiscount`, `createSettlement`, `createRefund` now use `findByIdAndOrganizationId` when the actor is non-admin. Cross-org mutations return 404. SYSTEM_ADMIN bypasses. |
| Content org-ownership | `ContentService.java`, `ContentController.java` | All content mutations (`updateItem`, `scheduleItem`, `publishItem`, `unpublishItem`) verify the actor's org matches the item's org. SYSTEM_ADMIN bypasses. `updateItem` now requires actor parameter. |
| Forced-dispatch integrity | `DispatchService.java`, `DispatchOrder.java`, `V4__dispatch_assigned_at.sql` | Only the designated driver may accept a `DISPATCHER_ASSIGNED` order (others get 403). New `assignedAt` column tracks assignment time. |
| 30-minute reassignment | `DispatchService.reassignOrder()`, `DispatchController.java`, `ReassignOrderRequest.java` | New `POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reassign` endpoint. Enforces 30-minute wait before a forced-dispatch order can be reassigned to another driver. |
| Event notifications | `BillingService.java`, `ContentService.java` | Late-fee and discount events notify org users. Publish/unpublish events notify content creator. All offline/in-app only. |
| Report-grade handover | `PaymentService.processHandover()`, `ShiftHandoverSummary.java` | Handover endpoint now returns a full `ShiftHandoverSummary` with submitted/posted breakdowns by method, delta, and discrepancy case details. |

### New Dispatch Endpoint: Reassign Order

```
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reassign
Roles: DISPATCHER, SYSTEM_ADMIN
Headers:
  Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
Body:
{
  "newDriverId": "<uuid>",
  "entityVersion": <int>
}
```

Returns 422 if the order is not in ASSIGNED state or if fewer than 30 minutes have elapsed since the original assignment.

## Testing

From the `repo` directory:

- **Git Bash / Linux / macOS:** `./run_tests.sh`
- **Windows PowerShell:** `.\run_tests.ps1`

The script waits for the API on the **host** port (default **18080**, same as `docker compose`). If you set `HOST_APP_PORT` when starting Compose, export the same value before running the shell script (PowerShell reads `HOST_APP_PORT` automatically).

## Architecture

- Spring Boot 3.2 + Java 17
- PostgreSQL 16 - all persistent state, money as integer cents, UUIDs as PKs
- Flyway - database migrations on startup
- Quartz - JDBC job store for scheduled/recurring tasks
- Spring Security - HTTP Basic auth backed by DB users
- Optimistic Locking - @Version on Bill, Payment, DispatchOrder, ContentItem, Comment

To inspect or restore a backup, attach to the volume or copy a file out:

```bash
# List available snapshots
docker run --rm -v civicworks_backups:/backups alpine ls /backups

# Restore a specific snapshot (replace <file> with the desired filename)
docker run --rm -v civicworks_backups:/backups postgres:16-alpine \
  sh -c "gunzip -c /backups/<file> | psql -h db -U civicworks civicworks"
```

## Final Fixes (Acceptance Blockers)

The following changes were made to resolve final acceptance blockers:

| Blocker | File(s) changed | Why |
|---------|----------------|-----|
| Flyway duplicate DDL | `V1__initial_schema.sql`, `V3__extensions.sql` | V1 and V3 both defined `notification_outbox`, causing Flyway to fail on every fresh DB. V1's definition removed; V3 is the single source of truth with `IF NOT EXISTS` guard. |
| Dispatch bypass via missing pickup coords | `CreateDispatchOrderRequest.java`, `DispatchService.java` | `pickupLat`/`pickupLng` are now `@NotNull` at order creation. `acceptOrder` rejects with 400 if the order has no pickup location, closing the last bypass path. |
| API test contract drift | `API_tests/tests/test_dispatch.py` | Accept-order tests updated to include required `driverLat`/`driverLng` body; new negative test asserts 400 for missing driver coords; new test asserts 422 for missing pickup coords at order creation. |
| SYSTEM_ADMIN global billing scope | `BillingController.java` | Replaced `actor.getOrganization() != null ? orgId : null` with an explicit role check: `SYSTEM_ADMIN` always passes `null` orgId (global), regardless of whether the account has an org set. |
| Template-driven reminder notifications | `NotificationTemplateService.java` (new), `ReminderNotificationJob.java` | Hardcoded subject/body strings in the scheduler replaced with a `{{placeholder}}`-based template engine. `OVERDUE_BILL_REMINDER` is a built-in template; new templates can be registered at runtime. |

## Security and Integrity Fixes

| Fix | File(s) changed | Why |
|-----|----------------|-----|
| Billing tenant isolation | `BillingService.java`, `PaymentService.java` | `applyLateFee`, `applyDiscount`, `createSettlement`, `createRefund` now use `findByIdAndOrganizationId` when the actor is non-admin. Cross-org mutations return 404. SYSTEM_ADMIN bypasses. |
| Content org-ownership | `ContentService.java`, `ContentController.java` | All content mutations (`updateItem`, `scheduleItem`, `publishItem`, `unpublishItem`) verify the actor's org matches the item's org. SYSTEM_ADMIN bypasses. `updateItem` now requires actor parameter. |
| Forced-dispatch integrity | `DispatchService.java`, `DispatchOrder.java`, `V4__dispatch_assigned_at.sql` | Only the designated driver may accept a `DISPATCHER_ASSIGNED` order (others get 403). New `assignedAt` column tracks assignment time. |
| 30-minute reassignment | `DispatchService.reassignOrder()`, `DispatchController.java`, `ReassignOrderRequest.java` | New `POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reassign` endpoint. Enforces 30-minute wait before a forced-dispatch order can be reassigned to another driver. |
| Event notifications | `BillingService.java`, `ContentService.java` | Late-fee and discount events notify org users. Publish/unpublish events notify content creator. All offline/in-app only. |
| Report-grade handover | `PaymentService.processHandover()`, `ShiftHandoverSummary.java` | Handover endpoint now returns a full `ShiftHandoverSummary` with submitted/posted breakdowns by method, delta, and discrepancy case details. |

### New Dispatch Endpoint: Reassign Order

```
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reassign
Roles: DISPATCHER, SYSTEM_ADMIN
Headers:
  Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
Body:
{
  "newDriverId": "<uuid>",
  "entityVersion": <int>
}
```

Returns 422 if the order is not in ASSIGNED state or if fewer than 30 minutes have elapsed since the original assignment.

## Testing

From the `repo` directory:

- **Git Bash / Linux / macOS:** `./run_tests.sh`
- **Windows PowerShell:** `.\run_tests.ps1`

The script waits for the API on the **host** port (default **18080**, same as `docker compose`). If you set `HOST_APP_PORT` when starting Compose, export the same value before running the shell script (PowerShell reads `HOST_APP_PORT` automatically).

## Architecture

- Spring Boot 3.2 + Java 17
- PostgreSQL 16 - all persistent state, money as integer cents, UUIDs as PKs
- Flyway - database migrations on startup
- Quartz - JDBC job store for scheduled/recurring tasks
- Spring Security - HTTP Basic auth backed by DB users
- Optimistic Locking - @Version on Bill, Payment, DispatchOrder, ContentItem, Comment

# CivicWorks Community Services Backend

Offline-first municipal community services platform.

## Quick Start

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

The application is published on the host at **http://localhost:18080** by default (container still listens on 8080 inside the network). Another process on your machine may already use 8080; this mapping avoids that conflict. To use host port 8080 instead, run: `HOST_APP_PORT=8080 docker compose up` (PowerShell: `$env:HOST_APP_PORT=8080; docker compose up`).

The PostgreSQL database runs on host port **5432**.

## Default Credentials

All users have password `admin123`.

| Username   | Role           |
|------------|----------------|
| admin      | SYSTEM_ADMIN   |
| editor     | CONTENT_EDITOR |
| clerk      | BILLING_CLERK  |
| driver1    | DRIVER         |
| moderator  | MODERATOR      |
| dispatcher | DISPATCHER     |
| auditor    | AUDITOR        |

## API Base URL

`http://localhost:18080/api/v1` (or your `HOST_APP_PORT` if set)

## Authentication

HTTP Basic Auth on every request.

## Key Endpoints

### Content Management
- POST /api/v1/content/items - Create content item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- GET /api/v1/content/items - List content items (authenticated)
- PATCH /api/v1/content/items/{id} - Update content item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/content/items/{id}/publish - Publish item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/content/items/{id}/unpublish - Unpublish item (CONTENT_EDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN, MODERATOR)
- GET /api/v1/content/items/{id}/publish-history - Publish history (authenticated)

### Billing
- POST /api/v1/billing/fee-items - Create fee item (BILLING_CLERK, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/billing/billing-runs - Create billing run (BILLING_CLERK, SYSTEM_ADMIN)
- POST /api/v1/billing/bills/{billId}/late-fee/apply - Apply late fee (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/bills/{billId}/discount - Apply discount (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/bills/{billId}/settlements - Create settlement (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/payments/{paymentId}/refunds - Issue refund (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/shifts/{shiftId}/handover - Submit shift handover (BILLING_CLERK)
- POST /api/v1/billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve - Resolve discrepancy (BILLING_CLERK, SYSTEM_ADMIN)

### Dispatch
- POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders - Create order (DISPATCHER, DRIVER)
- POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/accept - Accept order (DRIVER)
- POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reject - Reject order (DRIVER)
- GET /api/v1/drivers/{driverId}/eligibility - Driver eligibility (DISPATCHER, DRIVER)

#### Create order — required fields

`pickupLat` and `pickupLng` are **required** at order creation.  Orders without a
pickup location cannot be accepted (the 3-mile distance check requires a trusted
server-side coordinate source).

```json
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders
{
  "zoneId":     "<uuid>",
  "mode":       "GRAB",
  "pickupLat":  37.77,
  "pickupLng":  -122.41,
  "dropoffLat": 37.78,
  "dropoffLng": -122.42
}
```

#### Accept order — required request body

`driverLat` and `driverLng` are **required** in the request body.  The server
performs a Haversine distance check and rejects the acceptance (HTTP 400) if
either the driver coordinates or the order pickup coordinates are missing, or if
the driver is more than 3 miles from the pickup location.

```
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/accept
Headers:
  X-Entity-Version: <version>          (required — optimistic locking)
  Idempotency-Key:  <uuid>             (required)
Body:
{
  "driverLat": 37.77,
  "driverLng": -122.41
}
```

### Search
- GET /api/v1/search/typeahead?q={query} - Full-text search (authenticated)

### Audit
- GET /api/v1/audit/logs - Audit logs (AUDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN)

## Backups

A `backup` service runs alongside the application in `docker-compose.yml`.
Every 24 hours it calls `pg_dump` against the `db` service and writes a
gzip-compressed snapshot to the `civicworks_backups` named Docker volume:

```
/backups/civicworks_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.sql.gz
```

**Retention policy:** backup files older than 7 days are automatically deleted
by the same container process (`find … -mtime +7 -delete`), so the volume
never accumulates more than ~8 snapshots under normal operation.

To inspect or restore a backup, attach to the volume or copy a file out:

```bash
# List available snapshots
docker run --rm -v civicworks_backups:/backups alpine ls /backups

# Restore a specific snapshot (replace <file> with the desired filename)
docker run --rm -v civicworks_backups:/backups postgres:16-alpine \
  sh -c "gunzip -c /backups/<file> | psql -h db -U civicworks civicworks"
```

## Final Fixes (Acceptance Blockers)

The following changes were made to resolve final acceptance blockers:

| Blocker | File(s) changed | Why |
|---------|----------------|-----|
| Flyway duplicate DDL | `V1__initial_schema.sql`, `V3__extensions.sql` | V1 and V3 both defined `notification_outbox`, causing Flyway to fail on every fresh DB. V1's definition removed; V3 is the single source of truth with `IF NOT EXISTS` guard. |
| Dispatch bypass via missing pickup coords | `CreateDispatchOrderRequest.java`, `DispatchService.java` | `pickupLat`/`pickupLng` are now `@NotNull` at order creation. `acceptOrder` rejects with 400 if the order has no pickup location, closing the last bypass path. |
| API test contract drift | `API_tests/tests/test_dispatch.py` | Accept-order tests updated to include required `driverLat`/`driverLng` body; new negative test asserts 400 for missing driver coords; new test asserts 422 for missing pickup coords at order creation. |
| SYSTEM_ADMIN global billing scope | `BillingController.java` | Replaced `actor.getOrganization() != null ? orgId : null` with an explicit role check: `SYSTEM_ADMIN` always passes `null` orgId (global), regardless of whether the account has an org set. |
| Template-driven reminder notifications | `NotificationTemplateService.java` (new), `ReminderNotificationJob.java` | Hardcoded subject/body strings in the scheduler replaced with a `{{placeholder}}`-based template engine. `OVERDUE_BILL_REMINDER` is a built-in template; new templates can be registered at runtime. |

## Security and Integrity Fixes

| Fix | File(s) changed | Why |
|-----|----------------|-----|
| Billing tenant isolation | `BillingService.java`, `PaymentService.java` | `applyLateFee`, `applyDiscount`, `createSettlement`, `createRefund` now use `findByIdAndOrganizationId` when the actor is non-admin. Cross-org mutations return 404. SYSTEM_ADMIN bypasses. |
| Content org-ownership | `ContentService.java`, `ContentController.java` | All content mutations (`updateItem`, `scheduleItem`, `publishItem`, `unpublishItem`) verify the actor's org matches the item's org. SYSTEM_ADMIN bypasses. `updateItem` now requires actor parameter. |
| Forced-dispatch integrity | `DispatchService.java`, `DispatchOrder.java`, `V4__dispatch_assigned_at.sql` | Only the designated driver may accept a `DISPATCHER_ASSIGNED` order (others get 403). New `assignedAt` column tracks assignment time. |
| 30-minute reassignment | `DispatchService.reassignOrder()`, `DispatchController.java`, `ReassignOrderRequest.java` | New `POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reassign` endpoint. Enforces 30-minute wait before a forced-dispatch order can be reassigned to another driver. |
| Event notifications | `BillingService.java`, `ContentService.java` | Late-fee and discount events notify org users. Publish/unpublish events notify content creator. All offline/in-app only. |
| Report-grade handover | `PaymentService.processHandover()`, `ShiftHandoverSummary.java` | Handover endpoint now returns a full `ShiftHandoverSummary` with submitted/posted breakdowns by method, delta, and discrepancy case details. |

### New Dispatch Endpoint: Reassign Order

```
POST /api/v1/dispatch/orders/{orderId}/reassign
Roles: DISPATCHER, SYSTEM_ADMIN
Headers:
  Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
Body:
{
  "newDriverId": "<uuid>",
  "entityVersion": <int>
}
```

Returns 422 if the order is not in ASSIGNED state or if fewer than 30 minutes have elapsed since the original assignment.

## Testing

From the `repo` directory:

- **Git Bash / Linux / macOS:** `./run_tests.sh`
- **Windows PowerShell:** `.\run_tests.ps1`

The script waits for the API on the **host** port (default **18080**, same as `docker compose`). If you set `HOST_APP_PORT` when starting Compose, export the same value before running the shell script (PowerShell reads `HOST_APP_PORT` automatically).

## Architecture

- Spring Boot 3.2 + Java 17
- PostgreSQL 16 - all persistent state, money as integer cents, UUIDs as PKs
- Flyway - database migrations on startup
- Quartz - JDBC job store for scheduled/recurring tasks
- Spring Security - HTTP Basic auth backed by DB users
- Optimistic Locking - @Version on Bill, Payment, DispatchOrder, ContentItem, Comment

* If absolute delta > $1.00, create a `discrepancy_case` and:
  * assign it to `BILLING_CLERK`
  * require `AUDITOR` oversight (read-only review + explicit sign-off)

Response:
```json
{
  "handover_id": "id",
  "discrepancy_case_id": "id|null",
  "status": "RECORDED|DISCREPANCY_OPEN"
}
```

`GET /billing/discrepancies?status=&from=&to=`

Requires `BILLING_CLERK` (and `AUDITOR` can view without mutation).

`POST /billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve`

Request:
```json
{
  "resolution": "APPROVED|REJECTED|NEEDS_MORE_INFO",
  "notes": "string|null",
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Requires `AUDITOR` to finalize sign-off.

Server behavior:
* Compare submitted totals against posted A/R for the shift.
* If absolute delta > $1.00, create a `discrepancy_case` and:
  * assign it to `BILLING_CLERK` for resolution
  * `AUDITOR` provides read-only oversight (can view discrepancy list and audit logs, but cannot mutate)

Response:
```json
{
  "handover_id": "id",
  "discrepancy_case_id": "id|null",
  "status": "RECORDED|DISCREPANCY_OPEN"
}
```

`GET /billing/discrepancies?status=&from=&to=`

Requires `BILLING_CLERK`, `AUDITOR`, or `SYSTEM_ADMIN`. `AUDITOR` has read-only access for review purposes.

`POST /billing/discrepancies/{caseId}/resolve`

Request:
```json
{
  "resolution": "APPROVED|REJECTED|NEEDS_MORE_INFO",
  "notes": "string|null",
  "entity_version": 0
}
```

Requires `BILLING_CLERK` or `SYSTEM_ADMIN`. Auditor oversight is read-only via the discrepancy list and audit log endpoints.
